company_type: Specialty Retail - Home Furnishings and Kitchenware

dependencies: Approximately 82% of merchandise purchases sourced from foreign suppliers
23% from China, 16% from India, 14% from Vietnam
Largest single supplier accounts for approximately 3% of purchases
Dependence on foreign suppliers and third-party agents for timely sourcing
Reliance on third-party carriers for merchandise shipments
risks: Global supply chain disruptions from labor disputes, union organizing activity, geopolitical instability, acts of terrorism, war, outbreaks of disease
Instability in Middle East deterring commercial vessels from Suez Canal, increasing transit times and costs
Disruptions in Panama Canal affecting shipping channels
Increasing labor costs in supplier countries
Shortages of raw materials used to make products
Supplier financial instability or going out of business
Failure of suppliers to adhere to quality control standards and global compliance program

regulatory_risks: Compliance with laws, rules and regulations of U.S. and multiple foreign jurisdictions
Changes in U.S. (federal, state and local) and international tax laws and trade policies
Impact of current and potential future tariffs
Export regulations related to franchise arrangements
Employment and labor law changes, including minimum wage and health care requirements
Data privacy regulations: GDPR (EU), CCPA and CPRA (California), CPPA (Canada)
16 effective comprehensive state privacy laws expected by end of 2025
Federal Trade Commission and state attorneys general proposing new AI-related laws
SEC Rules on Cybersecurity Risk Management, Strategy, Governance, and Incident Disclosure
Product safety and quality regulations, potential for increased recalls
OECD Pillar Two minimum tax regime (15% minimum tax) implemented in Canada, UK, Australia, Netherlands, Italy, Portugal, Vietnam
Sales and use tax, value-added tax, goods and services tax obligations

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance mentioned as regulatory risk
Geopolitical instability affecting global supply chain
War and fears of war impacting consumer spending and operations
Acts of terrorism affecting supply chain and operations
Instability in Middle East affecting shipping routes (Suez Canal disruptions)
